title: Add ping use-case and CLI command
description:
# Add ping use-case and CLI command
Feature: add-ping-command | Task 1 of 2

## Specification

Create `src/usecases/ping.ts` with a `ping()` function that returns:
- `version`: from `src/version.ts`
- `projectRoot`: the resolved directory
- `taskBackend`: current config value (fs or br)
- `integrations`: `{ br: boolean, bv: boolean, cass: boolean, agentMail: boolean }`

Create `src/commands/ping/run.ts` with:
- `meta.name: 'ping'`
- Calls `ping(services)` use-case
- Human output: `maestro <version> [ok]` + integration status lines
- JSON output via standard `output()` helper

Run `bun run src/commands/_internal/generate.ts` to register.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- `maestro ping` outputs version and integration status
- `maestro ping --json` outputs structured JSON
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun test` passes (no regressions)

**Depends on**: none

## Prior Work

- **unknown-maestro-vek**: Generated from plan.md task 2 for feature "add-ping-command"
- **unknown-maestro-1gu**: Generated from plan.md task 1 for feature "add-ping-command"

## Context (from research/discovery)

### discovery

# Discovery: add-ping-command

## Codebase findings

- Commands live in `src/commands/<domain>/<action>.ts` using citty `defineCommand`
- Registry auto-generated by `src/commands/_internal/generate.ts` -- scans command dirs, extracts `meta.name`
- Run `bun run src/commands/_internal/generate.ts` after adding new commands
- Status command (`src/commands/status/show.ts`) is the closest reference pattern -- calls a use-case, formats output
- `getServices()` provides all ports (taskPort, graphPort, handoffPort, searchPort, etc.)
- `output()` helper handles JSON vs human-readable formatting
- `handleCommandError()` for error handling
- `VERSION` from `src/version.ts`
- Integrations detected at service init time via `command -v` checks

## What ping needs

- CLI: `maestro ping` -- returns version, project root, config summary, integration availability
- MCP: `maestro_ping` -- same data as JSON for agents
- Use-case: simple function that gathers system info (no complex logic)
- No new dependencies needed
notes: Implemented ping use-case and CLI command. Creates src/usecases/ping.ts and src/commands/ping/run.ts. Verified: tests pass, build succeeds, ping and ping --json output correct.

title: Add ping MCP tool
description:
# Add ping MCP tool
Feature: add-ping-command | Task 2 of 2

## Specification

Add `maestro_ping` tool registration in `src/server/ping.ts`:
- Calls the same `ping()` use-case
- Returns JSON result
- Register in `src/server.ts`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- MCP tool `maestro_ping` returns version + integrations JSON
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun test` passes

**Depends on**: 1

## Non-Goals

- Health checking external services (just availability detection, not connectivity)
- Adding tests for ping (trivial command, existing test suite validates no regressions)

## Ghost Diffs

- `src/commands/_internal/registry.generated.ts` -- auto-regenerated (not hand-edited)
- No existing files modified except `src/server.ts` (add import + register call)

## Dependencies

- **1. Add ping use-case and CLI command** (`maestro-1e9-add-ping-use-case-and-cli-command`)

## Prior Work

- **unknown-maestro-vek**: Generated from plan.md task 2 for feature "add-ping-command"
- **unknown-maestro-1gu**: Generated from plan.md task 1 for feature "add-ping-command"

## Context (from research/discovery)

### discovery

# Discovery: add-ping-command

## Codebase findings

- Commands live in `src/commands/<domain>/<action>.ts` using citty `defineCommand`
- Registry auto-generated by `src/commands/_internal/generate.ts` -- scans command dirs, extracts `meta.name`
- Run `bun run src/commands/_internal/generate.ts` after adding new commands
- Status command (`src/commands/status/show.ts`) is the closest reference pattern -- calls a use-case, formats output
- `getServices()` provides all ports (taskPort, graphPort, handoffPort, searchPort, etc.)
- `output()` helper handles JSON vs human-readable formatting
- `handleCommandError()` for error handling
- `VERSION` from `src/version.ts`
- Integrations detected at service init time via `command -v` checks

## What ping needs

- CLI: `maestro ping` -- returns version, project root, config summary, integration availability
- MCP: `maestro_ping` -- same data as JSON for agents
- Use-case: simple function that gathers system info (no complex logic)
- No new dependencies needed
notes: Implemented maestro_ping MCP tool in src/server/ping.ts. Registered in server.ts. Returns version, projectRoot, taskBackend, integrations.

description:
# Task: maestro-3fg-create-a-test-file

## Feature: test-e2e-workflow

## Dependencies

_None_

## Plan Section

### 1. Create a test file
- Create a simple `test-artifact.txt` in the feature context directory
- Validates: task-start, worker prompt generation, task-finish
**Depends on**: none

## Task Type

testing

## Context

## discovery-notes

# Discovery Notes

## What
This is a test feature to validate the full maestro workflow end-to-end.

## Findings
- The CLI plugin responds correctly to status and feature-create calls
- Both mcp__maestro and mcp__hive plugins are available
- No existing features were present before this test

## Decisions
- Using a simple two-task plan to exercise the full lifecycle
- Will test: context-write, plan-write, plan-read, plan-approve, task-sync, task-start, task-finish, feature-complete

- Calls the same `ping()` use-case
- Returns JSON result
- Register in `src/server.ts`

- MCP tool `maestro_ping` returns version + integrations JSON
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun test` passes

# Discovery: add-ping-command

## Codebase findings

- Commands live in `src/commands/<domain>/<action>.ts` using citty `defineCommand`
- Registry auto-generated by `src/commands/_internal/generate.ts` -- scans command dirs, extracts `meta.name`
- Run `bun run src/commands/_internal/generate.ts` after adding new commands
- Status command (`src/commands/status/show.ts`) is the closest reference pattern -- calls a use-case, formats output
- `getServices()` provides all ports (taskPort, graphPort, handoffPort, searchPort, etc.)
- `output()` helper handles JSON vs human-readable formatting
- `handleCommandError()` for error handling
- `VERSION` from `src/version.ts`
- Integrations detected at service init time via `command -v` checks

## What ping needs

- CLI: `maestro ping` -- returns version, project root, config summary, integration availability
- MCP: `maestro_ping` -- same data as JSON for agents
- Use-case: simple function that gathers system info (no complex logic)
- No new dependencies needed
